MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Rose Marie Tavares, et al.
Shelley K. Tinkoff of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendants / opposing parties Union Carbide Corporation, Supro Corporation
Evanthia M. Spanos of BERRY & BERRY (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR AN ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF
Hearing held. The Court ruled as follows: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted per CCP 36(a). Trial is October 21, 2013 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day of trial is Oct. 23, 2013. Before any motions in limine are filed with the court the defendants are ordered to meet and confer on all motions in limine in order to avoid frivolous and duplicative hearings. If the parties do not meet and confer on the motions in limine the court will take appropriate actions. Defendants' joint list of MIL due by Oct. 17.
Last day for hearing on defendant Supro's summary motion is October 21, 2013, to be heard on 60 days notice. Last day for hearing on all other summary motions is October 17, 2013. Motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant sections of the C.C.P.
Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on all defendants no later than Sept. 5, 2013. Defendants' responses to the demand are due no later than Sept 12, 2013. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on Oct. 10, 2013.
Time to respond to written contention discovery is shortened to 20 days. Plaintiffs stipulate to produce all medical billings and payment records for the service of the providers. Fact discovery cut-off date is Oct. 4, 2013. Expert discovery cut off is Oct. 18, 2013 No later than July 11, 2013, plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than July 11, 2013, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. All parties to meet and confer regarding any other scheduling or other issue which any of them want to have included in the preference order.
A further Case Management Conference on Sept. 18, 2013 at 1:30pm in Dept. 503. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on Oct. 21, 2013 at 1:30pm in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by Oct. 17, 2013 at 2:00pm. to the Court. Counsel for plaintiffs to prepare an order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Kim Richardson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #5915 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
